title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Best Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani) at Home
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast!
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and cream, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a classic Indian delight perfect for any meal.

description: Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, is a quintessential Indian dish cherished for its incredibly rich and creamy tomato-based gravy. Succulent pieces of chicken are marinated and then simmered in a luscious sauce infused with aromatic spices, butter, and a touch of cream. This comforting and flavorful curry is a true delight, perfect for a special meal or a cozy dinner.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, to avoid overcrowding the pan. Cook until browned on all sides and mostly cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, remaining 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews. Carefully transfer the hot tomato mixture to a blender along with the soaked cashews. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. You may need to add a splash of water if the sauce is too thick. Pass the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pan for an extra smooth texture, discarding any solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, remaining 2 tablespoons butter, and heavy cream. Stir gently to combine. Crush the Kasoori Methi between your palms and add to the sauce. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors and the sauce to heat through. Adjust salt if needed.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with warm naan bread, basmati rice, or roti. Enjoy your homemade Butter Chicken!

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate overnight. You can also lightly char the chicken in a tandoor or under a broiler before adding to the sauce for a smoky flavor.
Soaking cashews is crucial for a smooth, rich sauce. If you dont have cashews, almonds (blanched and peeled) can be used as a substitute for creaminess.
Always pass the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve after blending for that restaurant-quality smooth texture, removing any fibrous bits from the tomatoes or cashews.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: The Ultimate Indian Lunch Delight

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in Hindi. This iconic Indian lunch recipe is a global sensation, celebrated for its rich, creamy texture, tantalizing aroma, and succulent pieces of chicken bathed in a velvety tomato-based sauce. Originating from the bustling kitchens of Delhi in the mid-20th century, it has evolved into a staple in Indian restaurants and homes worldwide, embodying the warmth and complexity of Indian cuisine. Whether you're new to Indian cooking or a seasoned enthusiast, mastering this dish will bring a truly authentic taste of India to your table.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani translates literally to "chicken with butter." It’s a classic Indian curry characterized by its mild, slightly sweet, and tangy flavor profile, thanks to a harmonious blend of tomatoes, cream, and a medley of aromatic spices. Unlike some spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken is renowned for its approachable flavor, making it a favorite for all ages. The chicken is typically marinated in yogurt and spices, then grilled or pan-fried until tender before being simmered in the luxurious makhani sauce. This sauce is a masterpiece of balance, made from puréed tomatoes, cashews (for richness and thickness), ginger, garlic, and a unique combination of spices like garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), and a generous amount of butter and cream. It’s the perfect Indian lunch recipe, offering a comforting yet exotic experience.

## Ingredients You Need

To create the magic of Butter Chicken in your own kitchen, here’s a breakdown of the essential ingredients:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into bite-sized pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Makhani (Butter Chicken) Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 fresh large tomatoes, puréed
*   1/2 cup cashew nuts, soaked in hot water for 15 minutes (for creaminess)
*   1/2 cup water
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1 tablespoon sugar (optional, to balance tanginess)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating this delightful Butter Chicken is a rewarding process. Follow these steps for a perfect meal:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a little oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove the chicken and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, melt the butter with the remaining oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Add Tomatoes and Spices:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or puréed fresh tomatoes), red chili powder, ground coriander, turmeric powder, and salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the oil starts to separate.
5.  **Blend for Smoothness:** Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture. Transfer the entire sauce base to a blender (or use an immersion blender) and blend until perfectly smooth. This step is crucial for the signature velvety texture of Butter Chicken.
6.  **Finish the Sauce:** Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Add 1/2 cup of water and bring it to a gentle simmer. Stir in the garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, and sugar (if using).
7.  **Combine Chicken and Sauce:** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering sauce. Let it cook together for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce.
8.  **Enrich with Cream:** Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Cook for just a minute or two more, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil rapidly after adding the cream, as it can curdle.
9.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Your homemade Butter Chicken is ready!

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Marinate Longer:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight refrigeration is highly recommended.
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder:** For that vibrant red-orange color without excessive heat, use Kashmiri chili powder. It's milder and imparts a beautiful hue.
*   **Soak Cashews:** Don't skip soaking the cashews. It helps them blend into a super-smooth paste, contributing significantly to the sauce's creaminess and body.
*   **Blend Thoroughly:** A truly smooth sauce is key. Blend the sauce base until absolutely no lumps remain. A high-speed blender or an immersion blender works wonders.
*   **Kasoori Methi is Essential:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are a signature flavor in Butter Chicken. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste and adjust. If your tomatoes are very tart, a pinch of sugar can balance the flavors beautifully. Add salt and chili powder to your preference.
*   **Resting Time:** Like many curries, Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld and deepen.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a complete meal in itself, but its rich sauce begs to be soaked up by delicious accompaniments. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, fluffy naan is the quintessential pairing. Garlic naan or butter naan are particularly delightful for scooping up every last drop of the creamy sauce.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or a fragrant jeera (cumin) rice provides a perfect canvas for the rich curry.
*   **Roti or Chapati:** For a healthier option, whole wheat roti or chapati works wonderfully.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon juice) offers a refreshing contrast to the richness of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based raita (cucumber or mint) can also provide a pleasant counterpoint to the curry.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is undeniably delicious, you can easily adapt the recipe to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables (potatoes, peas, carrots). Follow the same marinating and cooking process for the paneer/tofu, or add vegetables directly to the sauce.
*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream. You can substitute some of the heavy cream with low-fat yogurt or coconut milk for a dairy-free option. Use a smaller amount of cashews or omit them if you prefer.
*   **Spicier Kick:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce.
*   **Dairy-Free:** Use oil instead of butter, and substitute heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or a cashew cream made from blended cashews and water.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic tandoori flavor, you can grill the marinated chicken on a charcoal grill or under a broiler until slightly charred before adding it to the sauce.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Its comforting flavors and luxurious texture make it an unforgettable Indian lunch or dinner. With this detailed guide, you're now equipped to create this culinary masterpiece in your own kitchen and savor the taste of India, one creamy bite at a time. Enjoy the journey of making and devouring this beloved curry!
